narrative:
The market is sitting comfortably in a business as usual regime, with a stress score of 15 out of 100 confirming a calm backdrop. Equity returns are essentially flat on the week, with SPY up half a percent and a z-score near zero, suggesting no directional conviction in either direction. There is no meaningful divergence between asset classes to flag at this stage.

VIX at 16.1 sits at the 28th percentile of its ten-year history, indicating that options markets are pricing a below-average level of uncertainty, and the term structure remains in contango, which is the normal configuration consistent with orderly conditions. High yield spreads at 278 basis points are at just the 13th percentile of their five-year range, with a z-score of negative 0.97, and investment grade at 76 basis points carries a similarly compressed z-score of negative 1.07. Credit is not merely confirming the calm equity environment but is, if anything, leaning more complacent than equities.

Brazil equities via EWZ dropped 4.1 percent over five days, standing out as the sharpest single geographic move in the dataset, while consumer discretionary and communication services shed 4.0 and 3.6 percent respectively. Against that, technology surged 6.4 percent, energy added 3.0 percent, and broad emerging markets via EEM gained 2.2 percent, pointing to a rotation dynamic rather than broad risk-off pressure. The sector picture is split almost evenly, with five of eleven sectors in the red, so the weakness is concentrated rather than systemic.
